lead_profile_text: ABROAD profile — lives in Indonesia, back in UK for ~1 month before returning. 2-bed cottage on long-let for 4 years; most recent tenant left last week, property now vacant and unfurnished. Considering STL vs new long-term tenant (interested party already in play). Recurring maintenance issue: shower pump replaced 6 times in 4 years — electric shower installation recommended before listing. Setup Quote SQ-2026-054: furnished 2-bed, £5,480, break-even 5 months.
emotional_profile: Pragmatic and cost-focused. Time-pressured by UK return timeline (June 30 deadline aligns with offer expiry). Live LTL competitor offer creates genuine decision tension. Analytical — wants all-months comparison before deciding. Needs remote management reassurance above all.
green_light_types: Property vacant and ready — tenant just departed
Time-pressured by UK departure (June 30 deadline aligns with offer expiry)
STL vs LTL comparison: all 12 months STL net exceed LTL net (even September STL ~£800 vs LTL £644)
Special offer 13% + VAT made at meeting
Maintenance issue (shower) can be resolved as part of Stayful setup
Uplift: £1,098/month more via STL than LTL
green_light_trigger_phrases: I'm only here for about a month
I want to understand the costs properly
There's someone interested in renting it

top_objections.text: How do I manage the property when I'm in Indonesia? — core concern about remote hands-off operation
top_objections.zac_response: Stayful handles everything remotely — no physical presence needed. Slack channel, monthly statements 1st-5th, maintenance coordination up to £300 without approval. Full remote management model.

top_objections.text: What about the shower and water tank issues? — pump replaced 6 times in 4 years
top_objections.zac_response: Electric shower installation recommended before listing to permanently resolve recurring pump issue. Stayful can coordinate this as part of the setup process.

top_objections.text: How do the costs work? — wanting clarity on what she actually nets after all deductions
top_objections.zac_response: STL projected net £1,742/month. Costs: guest covers cleaning; owner covers mortgage £600, insurance, utilities, council tax, gas safety. Maintenance auto-threshold £300.

top_objections.text: There's a tenant interested in the property — competing LTL offer is live
top_objections.zac_response: All 12 months STL net exceed LTL net. Even September (lowest STL month ~£800) beats LTL net £644. Full year uplift: £1,098/month. Year 1 conservative (80%): £16,723 net vs LTL £7,722 net.

top_questions.text: How much more will STL generate vs long-let?
top_questions.answer: STL projected net £1,742/month (£20,904/year) vs LTL net £644/month (£7,722/year). Uplift: £1,098/month. Year 1 conservative at 80%: £16,723 net. Every month of STL exceeds LTL net — even September (STL ~£800 vs LTL £644).

top_questions.text: How does Stayful manage the property when I'm in Indonesia?
top_questions.answer: Fully remote operation — Slack channel updates, monthly statements 1st-5th, maintenance handled without owner presence. Maintenance up to £300 automatic; above that requires owner approval via message. No physical presence ever needed.

top_questions.text: What about the recurring shower pump problem?
top_questions.answer: Electric shower installation recommended before listing to permanently resolve the pump replacement cycle. Stayful can coordinate this as part of the setup process.
Standard 15% + VAT. Special offer: 13% + VAT if agreement signed by 30 June 2026.

top_questions.text: What does the setup quote cover?
top_questions.answer: SQ-2026-054: Furnished 2-bed setup at £5,480. Break-even in approximately 5 months at projected income levels.

top_questions.text: What nightly rate and occupancy should I expect?
top_questions.answer: £205 nightly rate, 64% occupancy. Year 1 conservative projection at 80% of full performance: £16,723 net.

what_moved_them: Income projection showing all 12 months STL net above LTL net — even September at ~£800 vs LTL £644
Maintenance transparency — up to £300 handled without approval, nothing for Laura to manage from Indonesia
Stayful handles everything remotely — no physical presence ever required
Special offer 13% saving vs standard 15% — concrete benefit
what_fell_flat: Competing LTL tenant offer still live — interested tenant represents a real alternative
Property currently unfurnished — setup investment required before listing

conversion_gaps.gap: Competing LTL tenant offer is live — real alternative to STL
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Emphasise all-months STL > LTL comparison. Quantify 5-year income gap: £13,182/year uplift x 5 years = £65,910 more via STL. LTL tenant may not materialise anyway.

conversion_gaps.gap: Property unfurnished — setup investment and coordination needed before launch
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Setup Quote SQ-2026-054 already prepared (£5,480, 5-month break-even). Stayful can coordinate full furnishing setup remotely. Frame as one-time investment.

conversion_gaps.gap: No follow-up call booked — June 30 deadline approaching
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Book a follow-up call before June 30. Offer expiry and UK departure date are the same — decision must happen before she returns to Indonesia.

offer_framing: 13% + VAT (vs standard 15%) — saving 2 percentage points — if agreement signed by 30 June 2026. Aligns with Laura's UK departure date back to Indonesia. Hard deadline, genuine urgency.
zac_income_intro: STL projected net £1,742/month versus LTL net £644/month. That's £1,098/month more — every single month. Even your lowest STL month (September, ~£800) beats what you'd get from a long-term tenant.
zac_transition_phrases: Even in the worst month — September — STL at ~£800 net beats long-let at £644 net
The shower pump issue is actually easy to fix permanently — we can coordinate an electric shower installation as part of setup
You never need to be here — the whole operation runs remotely through Slack and monthly statements
zac_reassurance_phrases: You don't need to be in the country for any of this to work
Maintenance up to £300 is automatic — you'll never get a call at 2am in Indonesia
Monthly statements on 1st-5th — you always know exactly what's happening
The June 30 deadline aligns with your departure — designed to give you time to decide before you leave
zac_analogies: Long-let is like renting a car for £644 a month when you could Airbnb the same car for £1,742 — and we do all the driving
